intro: C is engineered for system-level programming, demanding explicit low-level array manipulation and memory tracking for mathematical computations. R is an interpreted, vector-oriented language designed explicitly for statistical computing, data analysis, and graphical visualization. Data scientists migrate tasks from C to R to drastically accelerate exploratory data modeling, statistical algorithm deployment, and report rendering.

diffs.from: Static, minimalist scalar types mapped strictly to fixed hardware bit sizes.
diffs.to: Dynamic, high-level system where all core objects are inherently vector-based arrays.

diffs.from: Imperative, block-scoped syntax utilizing structural loops and explicit index targeting.
diffs.to: Functional, expressive syntax incorporating mathematical notations and native vector operations.

diffs.from: Total manual application allocations and structural cleanups via standard heap APIs.
diffs.to: Automated engine-level tracking utilizing automated memory allocation and garbage collection.

diffs.from: Low-level multi-threading constructs reliant on OS-specific scheduling layers.
diffs.to: Single-threaded execution core by default, utilizing external parallel backends (e.g., `parallel`, `future`).

diffs.from: Minimal interfaces restricted to foundational system input/output and simple memory buffers.
diffs.to: Vast collection of embedded statistical formulas, linear algebra solvers, and matrix manipulation tooling.

examples.title: Vector Multiplication Operations
examples.description: Replacing raw loops with native, highly-optimized vector expressions.

examples.title: Linear Regression Computation
examples.description: Replacing complex numerical algorithm implementations with built-in high-level modeling functions.

tips.title: Think in Vectors, Not Scalar Loops
tips.body: Avoid using structural loops like `for` or `while` inside R. Vectorized functions process complete data arrays far faster by leveraging internal optimized compiled loops.

tips.title: Leverage CRAN Package Repository Ecosystem
tips.body: Do not code analytical workflows from scratch. Explore extensive visualization and modeling extensions like `tidyverse` and `data.table` for production data transformations.

tips.title: Mind the 1-Based Indexing Standard
tips.body: Note that R data structures utilize 1-based indexing conventions. Accessing the initial element of a collection requires index pointer `1`, not index pointer `0`.

tips.title: Integrate Fast Execution Layers using Rcpp
tips.body: If heavy algorithms hit memory or execution ceilings in interpreted R, safely bridge the performance gap by utilizing `Rcpp` to invoke specialized C++ calculation subroutines.
